description: RAM is a comprehensive initiative designed to transition conventional farming practices to regenerative agriculture models. The scheme focuses on soil restoration, crop diversification, organic matter enhancement, integrated pest management, and water conservation. It promotes practices such as conservation tillage, crop rotation, intercropping, mulching, and agroforestry to build ecosystem resilience and combat climate change while maintaining agricultural productivity.
summary: The Regenerative Agriculture Mission (RAM) is a central sector scheme launched in 2025 to promote sustainable and regenerative farming practices across India. It aims to restore soil health, increase biodiversity, enhance carbon sequestration, and improve farmers' income through climate-resilient agricultural methods.
eligibility_criteria: Eligible farmers include: small and marginal farmers (landholding up to 2 hectares), general category farmers willing to adopt regenerative practices, farming communities in all agro-climatic zones, individual farmers and farmer groups/cooperatives, farmers with no chemical fertilizer application for the past 6 months, and farmers aged 18-65 years engaged in agricultural activities.
target_beneficiaries: Small and marginal farmers (priority: 60%), general farmers (25%), women farmers (35% mandatory coverage), scheduled castes and scheduled tribes (20% minimum), farmer producer organizations (15% allocation), agricultural laborers transitioning to farming, and landless agricultural workers.
benefits_provided: Direct support of ₹15,000-25,000 per hectare (₹10,000 first year, ₹5,000-15,000 subsequent years), free soil health testing through Soil Health Card scheme, subsidized seeds and saplings at 50% discount, training programs and field demonstrations, certification support for organic farming, crop insurance coverage, collateral-free loans up to ₹50,000 at 4% interest, market linkage support and guaranteed procurement channels, and carbon credit benefits distribution.
application_process: Step 1: Register on the official RAM portal (ram.agrigovernance.in) or visit nearest block agriculture office. Step 2: Complete online application form with personal, land, and farming details. Step 3: Submit required documents (physical or digital). Step 4: Get soil health testing done through authorized labs. Step 5: Farm inspection and eligibility verification by agriculture department. Step 6: Approval and first installment release within 30 days. Step 7: Attend mandatory training and monitoring visits. Step 8: Subsequent disbursements based on performance metrics and field evaluations (quarterly or bi-annual).
documents_required: Aadhaar card and proof of identity, land ownership documents (Patta, Khata, Land Certificate, or lease agreement), soil health card (compulsory), bank account details and cancelled cheque, PWD certificate (if applicable), farmer registration number (e-NAM if available), photographic proof of land possession, and declaration of willingness to adopt regenerative practices for minimum 3 years.
keywords: Regenerative Agriculture, Soil Health, Sustainability, Carbon Sequestration, Crop Diversification, Climate Action, Organic Farming, Farmer Income, Ecosystem Restoration, Water Conservation, Agroforestry, Biodiversity, Central Sector Scheme, Sustainable Development Goals (SDG), Green Agriculture, Conservation Farming, Sustainable Livelihoods.

description: UFSP is a Central Sector Digital Service Integration scheme designed to create a one-stop digital platform for Indian farmers. The platform consolidates services from multiple agencies including state agriculture departments, NABARD, agricultural universities, e-commerce portals, and input suppliers. It leverages advanced technologies including AI, IoT sensors, and mobile-first interfaces to deliver real-time advisory, market linkage, weather information, soil testing results, and seamless access to credit and insurance products. The platform supports 22 official languages and operates on a decentralized architecture ensuring data security and compliance with data localization norms.
summary: The Unified Farmer Service Platform (UFSP) is a comprehensive digital initiative launched in 2024 to integrate multiple agriculture-related services under a single digital ecosystem. It aims to streamline access to government schemes, market information, extension services, and financial products for farmers across India.
eligibility_criteria: All registered farmers holding valid land ownership documents or lease agreements recognized by state agriculture departments. Tenant farmers with proof of cultivation rights are eligible. No income ceiling restrictions apply. Farmers engaged in crop production, horticulture, floriculture, and allied agricultural activities qualify. Special provisions for women farmers, SC/ST farmers, marginal and small holding farmers, and farming communities in aspirational districts.
target_beneficiaries: Approximately 150 million registered farmers across India. Priority focus on 5 million small and marginal farmers in 500 aspirational districts. Special outreach to women-led farming collectives (estimated 25 million beneficiaries). Youth farmers under 35 years (estimated 20 million). Farmers in geographically challenged regions including hilly, tribal, and coastal areas (estimated 30 million). Progressive farmers and farmer producer organizations (estimated 2 million).
benefits_provided: 1. Free access to personalized crop advisory based on soil health, weather patterns, and pest forecasting. 2. Real-time market price information and direct buyer linkage reducing intermediaries. 3. Instant verification and application for 50+ central and state government schemes in simplified digital format. 4. Seamless integration with digital payment systems for subsidy disbursement. 5. Access to certified seeds, inputs, and equipment from verified suppliers with quality guarantees. 6. One-click loan application to partner banks with 80% processing automation. 7. Consolidated view of insurance coverage and claim status. 8. Free soil testing reports and laboratory linkage with 2,000+ testing centers. 9. Capacity building through video tutorials, webinars, and training modules in local languages. 10. Farmer-to-farmer knowledge sharing network and success story documentation.
application_process: Step 1: Online registration via UFSP website or mobile app using Aadhaar-verified credentials or land ownership documents. Step 2: Basic profile creation including land holding size, crops grown, annual turnover, and banking details. Step 3: Email/SMS verification and KYC completion. Step 4: Selection of preferred services and schemes of interest. Step 5: Auto-eligibility assessment for government schemes using integrated database of farmer records. Step 6: One-time digitization of land documents through blockchain-secured uploads. Step 7: Activation of personalized dashboard with scheme recommendations and advisory feeds. Offline support: Community resource centers in 5,000+ gram panchayats with trained facilitators for digital literacy and assisted registration. No application fees charged.
documents_required: 1. Aadhaar card (mandatory for KYC and subsidy linkage). 2. Land ownership proof - either patta/mutation record from state revenue department OR lease agreement duly registered with local authority. 3. Bank account details with IFSC code and account holder name matching government records. 4. Recent passport-sized photograph (digital format). 5. Mobile number linked to Aadhaar for OTP verification. 6. Soil health card (if available, optional but recommended). 7. For women farmers: Self-declaration of gender designation or official identity document. 8. For tenant farmers: Registered lease deed and landlord affidavit. 9. For Farmer Producer Organization members: FPO registration certificate. All documents accepted in digital format through e-KYC process; physical submission not required.
keywords: Digital agriculture, Unified platform, Farmer services, Service integration, E-agriculture, Digital ecosystem, Agricultural technology, Market linkage, Crop advisory, Soil health, Digital literacy, Farmer empowerment, Input management, Price information, Agricultural financing, Government schemes, Digital subsidy, Farmer database, IoT sensors, Weather forecasting, Pest management, Blockchain verification, Central sector scheme, Marginalized farmers, Rural digital infrastructure

description: The Agroforestry Scheme is designed to promote the integration of trees with crops and livestock on the same piece of land. This multifunctional land use system helps in diversifying farm income, improving soil fertility, reducing dependence on chemical inputs, enhancing groundwater availability, and mitigating climate change impacts. The scheme provides financial assistance and technical support to farmers across all regions of India for establishing agroforestry models suitable to their agro-climatic zones. Farmers can select from various tree species including timber species (Teak, Sal, Shisham), fruit-bearing species (Mango, Guava, Ber), fodder species (Moringa, Subabul), and multipurpose species that provide multiple benefits.
summary: The Agroforestry Scheme (AFS) is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme launched in 2016 by the Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare to promote sustainable land use practices. The scheme encourages farmers to integrate trees with agricultural and pastoral activities on farmland, thereby increasing productivity, improving soil health, and generating additional income streams while contributing to environmental sustainability and carbon sequestration.
eligibility_criteria:
1. All individual farmers including marginal, small, and medium farmers irrespective of caste, creed, or religion
2. Joint Forest Management Groups (JFMG) and cooperative societies of farmers
3. Individual landholders with minimum 0.1 hectares of cultivable land
4. No upper limit on land holdings
5. Farmers must own or have cultivable rights over the land for minimum 10 years
6. Non-eligibility: Government and institutional lands, degraded forests, and water bodies
7. Preference given to farmers in drought-prone and rainfed areas
8. SC/ST farmers receive enhanced assistance and priority consideration
target_beneficiaries: Primary: Small and marginal farmers, landless agricultural laborers through community participation, farmers in rainfed and drought-prone regions. Secondary: Women farmers (minimum 30% participation target), youth farmers, agricultural communities in hilly and tribal areas. Estimated reach: Approximately 5-6 million farmers across India over the scheme period with focus on 15-20 million hectares of land.
benefits_provided:
1. Financial Assistance: Rs. 6,000-10,000 per hectare depending on tree density and species selected
2. Subsidy Coverage: 50% of cost for general farmers, 75% for SC/ST farmers and in hilly/tribal areas
3. Technical Guidance: Free advisory services and training on agroforestry practices
4. Input Support: Provision of quality saplings and seeds at subsidized rates
5. Livestock Benefits: Integration of fodder trees increases animal feed availability
6. Soil Health: Improved soil structure, nutrient cycling, and water retention
7. Income Generation: Multiple income streams from timber, fruit, fodder, and non-timber forest products (NTFPs)
8. Environmental: Carbon sequestration, reduced atmospheric CO2, groundwater recharge
9. Risk Mitigation: Crop diversification reduces farming risk
10. Market Support: Linkages with timber and produce buyers, value-added product opportunities
application_process:
1. Registration Phase: Farmers register with nodal agency (State Agricultural Department/Forest Department) through online portal or offline application centers
2. Verification: Ground truthing and verification of land ownership by district officials
3. Site Assessment: Soil and climatic assessment conducted to recommend suitable tree species
4. Training: Mandatory awareness and capacity building training for registered farmers
5. Plan Preparation: Customized agroforestry plan prepared with farmer participation
6. Approval: District Level Scheme Committee reviews and approves applications
7. Fund Release: First installment released upon plan approval, subsequent installments upon verification
8. Implementation: Farmers plant trees and maintain records of survival rates
9. Monitoring: Regular monitoring over 5-year implementation period with minimum 80% survival target
10. Completion Certificate: Issued after successful implementation and maintenance period
11. Payment Mode: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to farmer's bank account
documents_required:
1. Aadhar Card or any valid identity proof
2. Proof of citizenship (Ration card/Voter ID)
3. Land ownership documents or lease deed (minimum 10 years validity)
4. Land survey record (Khasra-Khatauni) from local revenue department
5. Proof of address
6. Bank account details with passbook copy
7. Photograph (2x2 passport size) - 2 copies
8. Caste certificate (for SC/ST applicants)
9. Land consolidation certificate (if applicable)
10. Undertaking regarding land use and non-conversion for 10 years
11. Group membership certificate (for groups/cooperatives)
12. Proposed agroforestry plan/layout prepared by local forestry department
keywords: Agroforestry, Tree farming, Sustainability, Carbon sequestration, Soil health, Income diversification, NTFP, Rainfed agriculture, Environmental conservation, Farmers welfare, Sustainable land use, Timber cultivation, Fodder production, Biodiversity, Climate change mitigation

description: Agri Startup India is designed to nurture and scale agricultural startups that address critical challenges in Indian agriculture. The scheme provides a comprehensive ecosystem for entrepreneurs to develop innovative solutions in areas such as precision agriculture, farm mechanization, agricultural biotechnology, supply chain management, agri-tech platforms, organic farming, and food processing. The scheme supports startups at various stages of development from ideation to scaling, with particular focus on youth entrepreneurs and women-led enterprises. It operates through a network of incubation centers, accelerators, and funding partners across India, facilitating technology transfer and market linkages.
summary: Agri Startup India (ASI) is a central sector scheme launched in 2023 by the Department of Agriculture & Cooperation under the Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare. The scheme aims to promote innovation and entrepreneurship in the agricultural sector by providing financial support, mentorship, and incubation services to agricultural startups engaged in value addition, agricultural technology, organic farming, horticulture, and allied activities.
eligibility_criteria: Eligible applicants include: (1) Indian citizens aged 18-65 years with entrepreneurial intent; (2) Registered startups (as per Startup India definition) in agriculture and allied sectors; (3) Startups incorporated within the last 10 years; (4) Business entities with minimum 51% stake held by Indian nationals; (5) Enterprises engaged in agricultural innovation, value addition, processing, or agri-tech solutions; (6) Priority given to women entrepreneurs, SC/ST entrepreneurs, and startups from Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities; (7) Entities with prior registration under any relevant ministry/department.
target_beneficiaries: The primary beneficiaries include: (1) Agricultural entrepreneurs and agri-business startups; (2) Youth in rural and semi-urban areas; (3) Women entrepreneurs in agriculture; (4) SC/ST entrepreneurs and disadvantaged groups; (5) Agricultural cooperatives and farmer producer organizations engaging in value addition; (6) Technology enthusiasts and innovators in the agri-sector; (7) First-generation farmers and agricultural graduates; (8) Estimated to benefit approximately 10,000 startups over a 5-year period.
benefits_provided: The scheme provides: (1) Direct Financial Assistance: Up to ₹25 lakhs for setting up incubation centers and up to ₹10 lakhs as seed funding to selected startups; (2) Mentorship Support: Access to experienced mentors, industry experts, and domain specialists; (3) Incubation Services: Workspace, equipment, and infrastructure at subsidized rates in partner incubation centers; (4) Market Linkages: Connection with government procurement agencies, retailers, and institutional buyers; (5) Capacity Building: Training programs, workshops, and skill development in entrepreneurship and agricultural practices; (6) IP Protection: Financial assistance up to ₹2 lakhs for patent filing and intellectual property protection; (7) Networking Opportunities: Participation in startup summits, exhibitions, and industry events; (8) Preferential Access: Priority in government tenders and procurement for eligible startups; (9) Tax Incentives: Tax benefits as per Startup India guidelines; (10) Loan Facilitation: Assistance in accessing credit from partner financial institutions at concessional rates.
application_process: The application process consists of the following steps: (1) Registration: Online registration on the Agri Startup India portal (www.agristartupindia.gov.in) with basic applicant information; (2) Application Submission: Complete application form with all required documents uploaded on the portal; (3) Initial Screening: Verification of eligibility criteria and document authenticity by nodal agencies; (4) Evaluation: Assessment of business plan, innovation, market potential, and social impact by a selection committee; (5) Interview/Presentation: Shortlisted applicants present their business model before the selection committee (online or offline); (6) Final Selection: Merit-based selection with weightage on innovation, feasibility, and entrepreneurial qualities; (7) Approval and Disbursement: Once selected, applicants enter into a grant/assistance agreement; (8) Fund Release: Initial tranche of funds released upon signing the agreement, subsequent tranches based on milestones; (9) Compliance and Monitoring: Regular monitoring, quarterly progress reports, and compliance checks; (10) Grievance Redressal: Provision for appeal and grievance redressal through designated channels within 30 days.
documents_required: The following documents are required for application: (1) Identity Proof: Aadhaar, PAN, or Voter ID of the founder(s); (2) Business Registration: Certificate of Incorporation/Partnership Deed/Proprietorship Registration; (3) Startup India Certificate (if already registered); (4) Business Plan: Detailed business plan with financial projections for 3-5 years; (5) Project Proposal: Description of the innovation/solution, market analysis, and implementation timeline; (6) Educational Credentials: Educational qualification certificates of founder(s); (7) Bank Details: Cancelled cheque and bank account statement of the business entity; (8) Tax Documents: Goods and Services Tax (GST) registration or Tax Identification Number (TIN); (9) Collateral Documents: As per lending norms if applying for financial assistance; (10) Technical Specifications: For agri-tech startups, technical documentation and prototypes; (11) Environmental Clearance: If applicable, for activities requiring environmental approval.
keywords: Agricultural startups, agri-entrepreneurship, agricultural innovation, farm technology, agri-business, startup incubation, value addition, organic farming, agricultural processing, agri-tech solutions, entrepreneurship development, rural entrepreneurship, farm mechanization, supply chain, agricultural biotechnology, government grants, startup funding, farmer producer organizations, innovation ecosystem, sustainable agriculture

description: The AI in Agriculture Initiative (AIAI) is a comprehensive centrally-sponsored scheme designed to harness the power of artificial intelligence and machine learning to transform India's agricultural sector. The initiative focuses on developing AI-powered solutions for crop health monitoring, predictive analytics for weather and pest management, soil health assessment, precision farming guidance, and market intelligence. The scheme aims to bridge the technology adoption gap between large-scale farms and smallholder farmers through subsidized access to AI tools, training programs, and digital infrastructure development.
summary: A central sector initiative launched in 2024 to integrate Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning technologies across Indian agriculture for enhanced productivity, resource optimization, and sustainable farming practices.
eligibility_criteria: Registered farmer groups (minimum 50 farmers per group), agricultural cooperatives, agricultural start-ups with DPIIT recognition, FPOs (Farmer Producer Organizations) with at least 500 members, state agricultural departments, and KVKs (Krishi Vigyan Kendras). Individual farmers with landholding of 1 hectare and above in designated AI adoption zones are also eligible. Implementation partners must be registered with the Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare.
target_beneficiaries: Approximately 50 lakh farmers across 25 states and Union Territories in Phase 1. Special focus on smallholder farmers (50% of beneficiaries), marginal farmers in rainfed areas, and women farmers (minimum 40% allocation). Targeted expansion to all states by 2027. Also benefits agricultural scientists, researchers, AgriTech start-ups, and agricultural input dealers.
benefits_provided: Free access to AI-powered mobile applications for 3 years (thereafter at subsidized rates), 80% subsidy on adoption of AI-enabled agricultural equipment and sensors, free WiFi hotspot installation at village level, customized weather forecasting and pest alerts, digital crop health monitoring through computer vision, yield prediction models, soil nutrient mapping, price forecasting for major crops, capacity building through 40 hours of digital literacy training per farmer, integration with e-NAM for better market linkage, and support for last-mile connectivity through community resource centers.
application_process: Online registration through AIAI portal, SMS-based verification system, biometric enrollment at CSCs, community training sessions at designated centers, mobile app-based onboarding, WhatsApp integration for non-smartphone users, field coordinator support in vernacular languages, and dedicated helpline (1800-AIAI-FARM) available in 15 Indian languages.
documents_required: Aadhaar and land ownership/lease documents, farmer registration certificate or KCC account details, banking credentials for DBT, agricultural land survey records (7/12 extract), community certificate (for group applications), GSTIN for registered organizations, implementation plan and budget proposal (for partner organizations), and letter of intent from state agricultural department.
keywords: AI agriculture, precision farming, machine learning, crop monitoring, predictive analytics, digital farming, sensor technology, agricultural technology, farmer welfare, smart agriculture, sustainable farming, AgriTech, Government of India scheme, Ministry of Agriculture, rural technology adoption, AI-enabled services

description: The Carbon Credit Farming scheme is designed to create a structured mechanism where Indian farmers can monetize their sustainable agricultural practices. Under this scheme, farmers implementing approved climate-smart techniques such as zero-till farming, agroforestry, crop diversification, and biochar application are eligible to participate in carbon credit markets. The government acts as an enabling platform, providing technical assistance, certification, and market linkage support. Each farmer's carbon sequestration is measured, verified, and converted into tradeable carbon credits through standardized protocols aligned with India's Nationally Determined Contributions (NDC) and international carbon accounting standards. The scheme aims to demonstrate India's commitment to climate action while creating an alternative income source for agricultural communities.
summary: Carbon Credit Farming (CCF) is a centrally-sponsored scheme launched in 2024 to incentivize farmers and agricultural enterprises to adopt sustainable practices that reduce carbon emissions and sequester carbon. The scheme integrates carbon markets with agricultural development, enabling farmers to earn additional income through carbon credit generation while promoting climate-smart agriculture and environmental sustainability across India.
eligibility_criteria: Active farmers with operational landholdings registered under land records; farmers organized under cooperative societies or Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs); agricultural enterprises engaged in crop production on at least 0.5 hectares of land; farmers willing to adopt and maintain approved sustainable agricultural practices for a minimum period of 5 years; Indian citizens with valid Aadhaar and banking credentials; farmers without outstanding dues to government agricultural credit schemes; participants must demonstrate willingness to undergo technical training and third-party verification processes
target_beneficiaries: Small and marginal farmers; agricultural laborers organized in groups; women farmers and self-help groups engaged in agriculture; farmer producer organizations and agricultural cooperatives; tribal farmers and communities with traditional sustainable practices; youth entrepreneurs in agriculture; rural farmers in emission-intensive crop production regions; conservation-focused farming communities; farmers in carbon-rich soil regions suitable for biochar and carbon sequestration
benefits_provided: Direct income from carbon credits through verified sequestration sales (₹200-500 per credit depending on methodology); government incentive of ₹2,000-5,000 per hectare annually for first 3 years to support transition costs; free technical training and capacity building in sustainable practices; subsidized access to climate-smart inputs and equipment (30-50% subsidy); certified third-party verification at no cost to farmer; access to carbon credit trading platform and market linkages; reduced input costs through soil improvement and reduced chemical dependency; improved soil health and long-term farm productivity; premium pricing for carbon-neutral certified produce; recognition through national sustainability registry
application_process: Registration through Common Service Centers (CSCs) or State Agriculture Department offices; submission of required documents and land records; preliminary verification by block-level agriculture officer; enrollment in mandatory 5-day orientation and technical training program; baseline carbon assessment through government-assigned technical partner; activation on National Carbon Credit Farming Portal (NCCFP); implementation of approved sustainable practices as per selected methodology; periodic monitoring through satellite imagery and on-ground verification; annual third-party audit and verification by NABL-accredited agencies; carbon credit issuance and transfer to farmer's digital wallet; facilitation of credit sale through government or private carbon trading platforms
documents_required: Land ownership or tenancy documents (Jamabandi/Khasra-Khatauni or lease agreement); Aadhaar card and voter ID; bank account details with cancelled cheque; photograph (passport size); FPO/cooperative membership certificate (if applicable); soil testing report (baseline); basic farm sketch with area measurement; signed undertaking committing to 5-year scheme participation; training completion certificate (after mandatory orientation); annual production and practice documentation records
keywords: Carbon Credit Farming, carbon sequestration, climate-smart agriculture, carbon markets, sustainable farming, zero-till farming, agroforestry, soil carbon, biochar, farmer income, environmental sustainability, climate action, NDC targets, agricultural carbon credits, emission reduction, green farming

description: Smart Village Agriculture (SVA) is a centrally sponsored scheme launched in 2025 to transform rural agricultural infrastructure through technology-enabled smart farming practices. The scheme aims to modernize farming operations by integrating IoT devices, weather monitoring systems, soil health testing, precision irrigation, and digital farm management platforms in selected villages across India. It promotes sustainable agricultural practices while enhancing productivity and farmer income through data-driven decision-making.
summary: A centrally sponsored infrastructure scheme designed to equip rural farming communities with smart agricultural technologies including sensor networks, automated irrigation systems, digital soil monitoring, and farm management software to increase productivity and sustainability.
eligibility_criteria: Primary eligibility includes: Villages with minimum 500 acres of cultivated agricultural land; Farmer cooperative societies or farmer producer organizations (FPOs) with minimum 200 members; State government nomination of participating villages; Minimum 70% small and marginal farmers in the village; Villages with basic electricity and internet connectivity infrastructure; Farmers with Aadhar enrollment; No previous beneficiary from similar technology-focused schemes in the last 3 years.
target_beneficiaries: Small and marginal farmers, agricultural laborers, women farmers, youth farmers (18-40 years), farmer producer organizations, agricultural cooperative societies, landless farmers through contract farming arrangements, and farming communities in aspirational districts with particular focus on eastern and central India.
benefits_provided:
1. Subsidized IoT sensors and soil monitoring equipment (80% subsidy up to Rs. 50,000 per hectare)
2. Smart irrigation systems with drip and sprinkler technology (75% subsidy)
3. Digital farm management application access (free for 5 years)
4. Weather forecasting and crop advisory services (real-time SMS/app-based)
5. Soil health testing kits and nutrient management guidance
6. Training and capacity building programs (minimum 40 hours per farmer)
7. Climate-smart agriculture certification
8. Market linkage through digital platforms
9. Renewable energy integration support (solar pumps, 60% subsidy)
10. Pest and disease monitoring through AI-enabled image recognition
application_process:
Step 1: Village selection by state government and registration on SVA portal (svascheme.gov.in)
Step 2: Individual farmer registration through common service centers (CSCs) or block office with necessary documents
Step 3: Verification by block agriculture officer and field inspection
Step 4: FPO formation or existing FPO strengthening (if required)
Step 5: Baseline assessment of land and existing infrastructure
Step 6: Technology need assessment and customized solution planning
Step 7: Sanction of subsidy and fund transfer to farmer accounts
Step 8: Installation of equipment and sensors with vendor coordination
Step 9: Training and handholding for 3 months post-installation
Step 10: Online monitoring and performance tracking through dedicated portal
documents_required:
1. Aadhar Card and voter ID of applicant
2. Land ownership certificate or lease deed (for at least 3 years)
3. Village eligibility certificate from Gram Panchayat
4. Bank account details and cancelled cheque
5. FPO/Cooperative society registration certificate (if applicable)
6. Land measurement report (pastal/mutation documents)
7. Caste certificate (for SC/ST farmers, if applicable)
8. Income certificate (for BPL/APL verification)
9. Farm map/sketch with GPS coordinates
10. Application form (available on official portal and at block office)
11. NOC from local irrigation department (if applicable)
keywords: Smart farming, IoT sensors, precision agriculture, digital agriculture, soil monitoring, smart irrigation, farm management software, climate-smart agriculture, farmer income, agricultural technology, sustainable farming, rural infrastructure, farmer producer organizations, agricultural innovation, smart villages, sensor networks, weather advisory, soil health, drip irrigation, agricultural digitization

description: The Blockchain Agri Traceability (BAT) scheme leverages distributed ledger technology to create immutable records of agricultural products throughout their supply chain. By implementing blockchain-based digital platforms, the scheme enables real-time tracking of produce quality, authenticity, and origin. It integrates primary producers, aggregators, processors, distributors, retailers, and consumers into a unified digital ecosystem. The scheme focuses on high-value crops including organic produce, spices, fruits, vegetables, and processed agricultural products. It promotes adoption of IoT sensors, QR codes, and smart contracts to automate verification and payment processes while ensuring complete transparency and reducing intermediaries.
summary: Blockchain Agri Traceability (BAT) is a central sector scheme launched in 2024 by the Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare to establish end-to-end traceability of agricultural produce through blockchain technology. The scheme aims to build consumer confidence, ensure food safety, enable fair pricing for farmers, and create transparent supply chains from farm to fork across India's agricultural ecosystem.
eligibility_criteria:
1. Agricultural cooperatives and farmer producer organizations (FPOs) with minimum 50 members
2. Individual farmers with operational landholding of 0.5 hectare or more engaged in produce meant for commercial sale
3. Registered agricultural exporters and APEDA-certified producers
4. Agricultural start-ups and agri-tech companies with blockchain implementation capability
5. Registered food processors and agricultural commodity aggregators
6. Government and private cold chain operators and logistics providers
7. All farmers are eligible regardless of caste, creed, or location
8. Preference given to women farmer groups and SC/ST cooperatives
target_beneficiaries: Approximately 25 lakh farmers directly and 100+ lakh consumers through supply chain improvements. Primary beneficiaries include small and marginal farmers in high-value crop growing regions, organized farmer groups, agricultural exporters, and consumers concerned with food safety. Secondary beneficiaries include agri-tech entrepreneurs, supply chain service providers, and retailers. Geographic focus includes major agricultural producing states: Karnataka, Maharashtra, Madhya Pradesh, Punjab, Haryana, Gujarat, Himachal Pradesh, and Uttarakhand.
benefits_provided:
1. Direct Income Benefits: Price premium of 5-15% on certified and traced produce; elimination of hidden costs through transparent pricing
2. Operational Efficiency: Reduced post-harvest losses (target 15-20% reduction); faster payment settlement through smart contracts (within 48 hours)
3. Market Access: Direct linkage to domestic and international buyers; access to premium export markets requiring traceability compliance
4. Risk Management: Early detection of crop issues; quality certification and insurance linkage
5. Consumer Benefits: Authentic product verification, nutritional and origin information, reduced food fraud
6. Environmental Impact: Sustainable farming practice tracking and verification; crop rotation and soil health data preservation
7. Data Ownership: Farmers retain ownership and control of their agricultural data
8. Subsidy Support: Up to 90% infrastructure cost subsidy for small farmers; up to 70% for other beneficiaries
application_process:
1. Registration Phase: Applicants register on the BAT portal (bat.agritech.gov.in) with basic farmer/organization details and land records
2. Verification: District Agricultural Department verifies credentials and land holding documents within 15 days
3. Technology Setup: Beneficiaries select approved blockchain service providers from government-empaneled list
4. Infrastructure Deployment: Installation of IoT sensors, QR code infrastructure, and mobile app setup (30-45 days)
5. Training: Mandatory 3-day training for farmers and operators on blockchain platform usage
6. Pilot Testing: 30-day pilot with 20-50 units of produce to test traceability workflow
7. Approval: State nodal agency approves full implementation after successful pilot
8. Subsidy Disbursement: Through Direct Bank Transfer in two tranches (50% on approval, 50% on successful pilot completion)
9. Monitoring: Continuous monitoring through portal dashboard with quarterly compliance checks
documents_required:
1. For Individual Farmers: Aadhaar card, voter ID/PAN, land ownership proof (Pattha/Patta/Registry), bank account passbook with IFSC code, mobile number, latest crop insurance policy (if applicable)
2. For FPOs/Cooperatives: Organization registration certificate, bylaws, list of members with Aadhaar numbers, bank account details, PAN of organization, resolution for scheme participation
3. For Exporters: APEDA registration, export license, GST certificate, bank details, technical team details for blockchain implementation
4. For Agri-startups: Company registration, GST certificate, MOA/AOA, bank account, technical specifications of blockchain solution, team qualification details
5. For All: Duly filled application form (Form BAT-01), declaration of authenticity, consent for data sharing, proof of operational address/contact details

description: IPM 2.0 represents the second generation of India's Integrated Pest Management program, evolved with modern technology integration. The scheme encompasses bio-pesticides promotion, pheromone traps, natural predator conservation, precision agriculture tools for pest monitoring, and real-time advisory systems. It targets farmers in identified pest-prone regions and provides comprehensive support including subsidized inputs, training, certification, and market linkages for organic/sustainable produce. The scheme integrates IoT sensors, AI-based pest prediction models, and mobile-based advisory platforms to enable data-driven pest management decisions.
summary: Integrated Pest Management 2.0 (IPM2.0) is a centrally sponsored agricultural scheme launched in 2024 to promote sustainable pest control practices across India. It aims to reduce chemical pesticide usage by 30-40% while maintaining crop productivity through eco-friendly integrated pest management techniques, digital monitoring systems, and farmer capacity building.
eligibility_criteria: Active farmers holding valid Kisan Credit Card or land ownership certificates; Agricultural cooperative societies with minimum 50 members; FPOs (Farmer Producer Organizations) with registered status; Farming communities in notified pest hotspot regions; Individual farmers or groups practicing agriculture for minimum 2 years; Priority to SC/ST farmers, women farmers, and marginalized sections; Age criteria: 18-65 years for individual applicants
target_beneficiaries: Small and marginal farmers (primary focus); Agricultural cooperatives and FPOs; Women farmer groups and self-help groups; Youth entering agriculture; Farmers in high-infestation districts; Estimated 25 lakh direct beneficiaries and 50 lakh indirect beneficiaries across India in pilot phase (expanding nationally)
benefits_provided: 50% subsidy on bio-pesticides and botanical products (up to Rs. 3,500 per hectare per season); 40% subsidy on pheromone traps and insect observation equipment; Free training and certification programs for IPM practitioners; Subsidized smart sensors and IoT devices at 35% of cost; Free pest monitoring and advisory services via SMS/WhatsApp; Guaranteed market linkage for certified organic/sustainable produce with 10-15% price premium; Crop insurance integration with 5% premium discount for IPM practitioners; Support for establishing community bio-factories for localized biopesticide production; Interest-free credit component for input purchases up to Rs. 50,000 per farmer
application_process: Step 1: Registration through Department of Agriculture website (www.agriculturescheme.gov.in) or common service center; Step 2: Submission of required documents (physical or digital copy); Step 3: Verification by village-level agriculture extension officer within 15 days; Step 4: Online approval by district agriculture department within 30 days; Step 5: Enrollment in designated training center nearest to farm location; Step 6: Completion of minimum 3-day IPM training module (online/offline options available); Step 7: Receipt of farmer ID and access to digital advisory platform; Step 8: Disbursement of first tranche subsidy (40%) to registered bank account after training completion; Step 9: Field monitoring by officials for 6 months; Step 10: Disbursement of second tranche (60%) upon successful implementation verification and submission of farm records. Application deadline: Year-round with seasonal windows (Kharif: June-July; Rabi: September-October)
documents_required: Aadhaar card or voter ID for identification; Land ownership certificate, land lease deed, or Kisan Credit Card; Bank account passbook (for fund transfers); Caste certificate (if claiming SC/ST benefit); Self-declaration form confirming farming as primary occupation; Agricultural extension officer verification; Geo-tagged farm photographs for area validation; Cooperative/FPO registration certificate (if applicable); Recent crop cultivation proof from previous season; Signed agreement for following IPM protocol for minimum 3 years
